stats.proposed_change: Increase the Texas residence homestead exemption under Tax Code §11.13(b) from $140,000 to $200,000 of appraised value.

nodes.conflict_rationale: The constitution fixes the school homestead exemption at $140,000. A statute increasing it to $200,000 would exceed constitutional authority, requiring a constitutional amendment first.
nodes.quote: The amount of $140,000 of the market value of the residence homestead of a married or unmarried adult, including one living alone, is exempt from ad valorem taxation for general elementary and secondary public school purposes.

nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces school district property tax revenue, altering local revenue levels used in §29.203(b) state aid calculations for public education grant students. Commissioner rules may need updating to reflect adjusted local revenue levels.
nodes.quote: If the district has a local revenue level greater than the guaranteed local revenue level but less than the level established under Section 48.257, a school district is entitled under rules adopted by the commissioner to additional state aid in an amount equal to the difference between the cost to the district of providing services to a student using a public education grant and the sum of the state aid received because of the allotment under Section 48.107 and money from the available school fund attributable to the student.

NOTICE OF BUDGET AND TAX RATE MEETING; BUDGET ADOPTION
nodes.conflict_rationale: The statute references homestead exemptions generically—'all homestead exemptions applicable in each year'—so no direct statutory conflict. However, comptroller-prescribed notice forms under Subsection (d) would require rulemaking updates to reflect the increased exemption in model calculations.
nodes.quote: after subtracting all homestead exemptions applicable in each year, other than exemptions available only to disabled persons or persons 65 years of age or older or their surviving spouses, and using the same group of residences for each year

nodes.heading: BOND AND TAX ELECTIONS
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces school district taxable value, affecting revenue available under the maintenance tax rate limits tied to taxable value and compressed rates referenced here.
nodes.quote: the maintenance tax rate per $100 of taxable value adopted by the district may not exceed the rate equal to the sum of $0.17 and the district's maximum compressed rate, as determined under Section 48.2551

nodes.heading: COMPONENTS OF MAINTENANCE AND OPERATIONS TAX
NOTICE OF FAILURE OR INABILITY TO PAY
STATE POLICY
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue for school districts, potentially affecting the neutrality standard of substantially equal access to similar revenue per student at similar tax effort. State funding adjustments would be needed to maintain compliance.
nodes.quote: The public school finance system of this state shall adhere to a standard of neutrality that provides for substantially equal access to similar revenue per student at similar tax effort, considering all state and local tax revenues of districts after acknowledging all legitimate student and district cost differences.

nodes.heading: PURPOSES OF FOUNDATION SCHOOL PROGRAM
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local school property tax revenue, potentially requiring rulemaking updates to ensure districts maintain adequate and equalized resources as outlined in this purpose section.
nodes.quote: adequate resources to provide each eligible student a basic instructional program

nodes.heading: USE OF FUNDING FOR CERTAIN PURPOSES
ADMINISTRATION OF THE PROGRAM
nodes.conflict_rationale: The commissioner must adopt rules to implement FSP calculations reflecting the increased homestead exemption amount, affecting local revenue determinations under the Foundation School Program.
nodes.quote: The commissioner shall adopt rules and take action and require reports consistent with this chapter as necessary to implement and administer the Foundation School Program.

nodes.heading: INCENTIVE FOR ADDITIONAL INSTRUCTIONAL DAYS FOR READING INTERVENTIONS
AVERAGE DAILY ATTENDANCE FOR DISTRICTS IN DISASTER AREA
OFF-CAMPUS PROGRAMS APPROVED FOR PURPOSES OF AVERAGE DAILY ATTENDANCE
DETERMINATION OF FUNDING LEVELS
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue for school districts, affecting the commissioner's state and local funding per-student determinations under §48.010(a). No statutory amendment needed, but rulemaking must account for reduced local revenue.
nodes.quote: the commissioner shall determine for each school district whether the estimated amount of state and local funding per student in weighted average daily attendance to be provided to the district under the Foundation School Program for maintenance and operations for the following school year is less than the amount provided to the district for the 2010-2011 school year

nodes.heading: COMMISSIONER AUTHORITY TO RESOLVE UNINTENDED CONSEQUENCES
NOTICE FOR SCHOOL DISTRICTS REGARDING RECOURSE FOR INVALID PROPERTY VALUES
BASIC ALLOTMENT
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local property tax bases, indirectly affecting tier one M&O tax rates (TR) and maximum compressed rates (MCR) that drive the basic allotment formula. No direct textual conflict, but downstream rulemaking under §48.2551 may need adjustment.
nodes.quote:
"TR" is the district's tier one maintenance and operations tax rate, as provided by Section 45.0032; and
"MCR" is the district's maximum compressed tax rate, as determined under Section 48.2551.

nodes.heading: ALLOTMENT FOR CERTAIN SPECIAL-PURPOSE SCHOOL DISTRICTS
SPECIAL EDUCATION TRANSITION FUNDING
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue, potentially affecting maintenance of local financial support under federal law referenced in subsection (a). The commissioner may need to adjust special education funding weights or amounts to compensate.
nodes.quote: the commissioner may adjust weights or amounts provided under Section 48.102 or 48.1021 as necessary to ensure compliance with requirements regarding maintenance of state financial support under 20 U.S.C. Section 1412(a)(18) and maintenance of local financial support under applicable federal law.

nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable appraised value, which affects the local share calculation under §48.256 referenced in the §48.203 DTR limitationformula, requiring consequential amendment to maintain rate alignment.
nodes.quote: the rate used to determine the district's local share under Section 48.256

nodes.heading: COMPUTATION OF AID FOR DISTRICT ON MILITARY RESERVATION OR AT STATE SCHOOL
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable property values, which feeds into the 'property value per student' computation under §48.204. The statute itself need not be amended, but commissioner rulemaking may be needed to update valuation methodologies.
nodes.quote: State assistance under this subchapter for a school district located on a federal military installation or at Moody State School is computed using the average tax rate and property value per student of school districts in the county, as determined by the commissioner.

nodes.heading: FINANCING; GENERAL RULE
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local ad valorem tax revenue for school districts, a financing source under §48.251(c)(2), requiring increased state funds under §48.251(c)(3) to cover FSP costs.
nodes.quote: (2) ad valorem tax revenue generated by local school district effort; and

nodes.heading: SCHOOL DISTRICT ENTITLEMENT FOR CERTAIN STUDENTS
ADDITIONAL STATE AID FOR TAX INCREMENT FINANCING PAYMENTS
ADDITIONAL STATE AID FOR AD VALOREM TAX CREDITS UNDER TEXAS ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT ACT
ADDITIONAL STATE AID FOR CERTAIN AD VALOREM TAX REFUNDS
ADDITIONAL STATE AID FOR ADJUSTMENT OF LIMITATION ON TAX INCREASES ON HOMESTEAD OF ELDERLY OR DISABLED
ADDITIONAL STATE AID FOR HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION
nodes.conflict_rationale: Statute already anticipates a 2025 homestead exemption increase and provides hold-harmless state aid. No structural conflict, but state aid calculations must be updated to reflect the new $200,000 amount and resulting revenue loss.
nodes.quote: Beginning with the 2025-2026 school year, a school district is entitled to additional state aid to the extent that state and local revenue under this chapter and Chapter 49 is less than the state and local revenue that would have been available to the district under this chapter and Chapter 49 as those chapters existed on September 1, 2024, if any increase in a residence homestead exemption under Section 1-b(c), Article VIII, Texas Constitution, as proposed by the 89th Legislature, Regular Session, 2025, had not occurred.

nodes.heading: STATE COMPRESSION PERCENTAGE
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces total taxable property value, affecting ECPV in the compression formula. No statutory amendment to this section is required, but the estimate under §48.269 must account for the change.
nodes.quote: "ECPV" is the estimated percentage change in total taxable property value for the applicable tax year as determined based on the estimate submitted to the legislature under Section 48.269.

nodes.heading: MAXIMUM COMPRESSED TAX RATE
nodes.conflict_rationale: The MCR formula in (b) depends on DPV (taxable property values). Increasing the homestead exemption reduces DPV. Subsections (d-3)–(d-6) only address the specific 2025 increase to $140,000; a further increase to $200,000 is not covered, requiring new statutory provisions for MCR calculation and commissioner adjustment authority.
nodes.quote: In calculating and making available school districts' maximum compressed rates under this section for the 2025-2026 school year, the agency shall calculate and make available the rates as if the increase in the residence homestead exemption under Section 1-b(c), Article VIII, Texas Constitution, as proposed by the 89th Legislature, Regular Session, 2025, took effect.

nodes.heading: LIMITATION ON MAXIMUM COMPRESSED RATE
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local school property tax revenue, which feeds into maximum compressed rate calculations under §48.2551 and the state compression percentage adjustments in §48.2552(c). Consequential amendments to these formulas may be needed to maintain intended compression outcomes.
nodes.quote: (c) The amount of revenue available to the state as a result of the differences in the amount of state aid and reduction in local revenue between calculating a district's maximum compressed rate in accordance with Subsection (b) and calculating the district's maximum compressed rate under Section 48.2551 shall be used to lower the state compression percentage under Section 48.255.

nodes.heading: PERMITTED TAX RATE FOR MAINTENANCE OF 2020-2021 SCHOOL YEAR BASIC ALLOTMENT
POSTING ON AGENCY WEBSITE OF INFORMATION RELATED TO REDUCTION OF LIMITATION OF TAX ON HOMESTEADS OF ELDERLY OR DISABLED
LOCAL SHARE OF PROGRAM COST (TIER ONE)
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable property values statewide, directly lowering DPV in the LFA formula. This shrinks each district's local share, shifting program costs to the state and likely requiring consequential FSP funding adjustments.
nodes.quote: "DPV" is the taxable value of property in the school district for the current tax year determined under Subchapter M, Chapter 403, Government Code.

nodes.heading: GUARANTEED YIELD INCREMENT ADJUSTMENT
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local school district property tax revenue, altering the per-cent-of-tax-effort and wealth-per-weighted-student calculations that drive the guaranteed yield increment adjustment. The agency must recalculate the adjustment amounts, but no statutory text amendment is required.
nodes.quote: the estimated cost to the state of maintaining the guaranteed level of state and local funds per weighted student per cent of tax effort under Section 48.202(a-1)(1) at the 96th percentile of wealth per weighted student for each year of the biennium

nodes.heading: LOCAL REVENUE LEVEL IN EXCESS OF ENTITLEMENT
ADJUSTMENT FOR RAPID DECLINE IN TAXABLE VALUE OF PROPERTY
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable values statewide, potentially triggering rapid-decline adjustments under §48.258(a). The commissioner may need rulemaking to address legislatively-caused declines versus other factors beyond board control.
nodes.quote: the commissioner shall adjust the taxable value of property in a school district that, due to factors beyond the control of the board of trustees, experiences a rapid decline in the tax base used in calculating taxable values in excess of four percent of the tax base used in the preceding year.

nodes.heading: ADJUSTMENT FOR OPTIONAL HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the mandatory homestead exemption under §11.13(b) reduces school district taxable values, affecting funding calculations under §403.302(d)(2), Gov't Code, referenced throughout this section. Rulemaking updates may be needed to reflect revised taxable value computations, though the section addresses §11.13(n) optional exemptions specifically.
nodes.quote: the commissioner may not provide funding under this chapter or Chapter 46 based on a school district's taxable value of property computed in accordance with Section 403.302(d)(2), Government Code

nodes.heading: ADJUSTMENT FOR PROPERTY VALUE AFFECTED BY STATE OF DISASTER
ADJUSTMENTS FOR CERTAIN DISTRICTS RECEIVING FEDERAL IMPACT AID

EXCESS FUNDS FOR VIDEO SURVEILLANCE OF SPECIAL EDUCATION SETTINGS
DISTRIBUTION OF FOUNDATION SCHOOL FUND
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable property values statewide, directly lowering tier one local share and shifting costs to state entitlements under §48.266's distribution formulas, requiring consequential fiscal adjustments and appropriations.
nodes.quote: the commissioner shall reduce the entitlement of each district that has a final taxable value of property for the second year of a state fiscal biennium that is higher than the estimate under Section 48.269 or the General Appropriations Act, as applicable.

nodes.heading: ADJUSTMENT FOR RESOLUTION OF DISPUTE OR ERROR RESULTING IN TAXATION OF SAME PROPERTY BY MULTIPLE SCHOOL DISTRICTS
ESTIMATES REQUIRED
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces total taxable property value. The comptroller's biennial estimate of total taxable value under §48.269(a)(2) must incorporate the new exemption amount, requiring a methodology update but no statutory amendment.
nodes.quote: the comptroller shall submit to the legislature an estimate of the total taxable value of all property in the state

nodes.heading: RECOVERY OF FUNDS FROM EXCESSIVE TAXATION
FOUNDATION SCHOOL FUND TRANSFERS
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable property values, which directly affects 'wealth per student' under §48.273(a)(4), potentially reclassifying districts among Categories 1–3 and altering their payment installment schedules.
nodes.quote: "Wealth per student" means the taxable property values reported by the comptroller to the commissioner under Section 48.256 divided by the number of students in average daily attendance.

nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue, altering current-year funding calculations under Chapters 48 and 49 referenced in §48.285(a). The retention allotment aid formula depends on current-year funding amounts, requiring rulemaking adjustments to account for reduced local revenue.
nodes.quote: the funding the district is entitled to under this chapter and Chapter 49 for the current year

COMPTROLLER AND APPRAISAL DISTRICT COOPERATION
nodes.conflict_rationale: No direct conflict, but increasing the homestead exemption amount requires appraisal districts and the comptroller to update cooperative implementation procedures to reflect the new $200,000 threshold.
nodes.quote: The chief appraiser of each appraisal district and the comptroller shall cooperate with the commissioner and school districts in implementing this chapter.

nodes.heading: RULES
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local school district property tax revenue, requiring commissioner rulemaking adjustments to Chapter 48 funding elements to match appropriation levels.
nodes.quote: The rules may provide for the commissioner to make necessary adjustments to the provisions of Chapter 48, including providing for the commissioner to make an adjustment in the funding element established by Section 48.202

nodes.heading: COMMISSIONER TO APPROVE SUBSEQUENT BOUNDARY CHANGES
HOMESTEAD EXEMPTIONS
nodes.conflict_rationale: Educ. Code §49.008 lets consolidated school districts adopt and set homestead exemption amounts under Tax Code §11.13 and prevails over inconsistent §11.13 provisions. Raising the base §11.13(b) amount requires conforming rulemaking guidance for consolidated districts.
nodes.quote: This section prevails over any inconsistent provision of Section 11.13, Tax Code, or other law.

nodes.heading: TRANSITIONAL ELECTION DATES: INCREASED HOMESTEAD EXEMPTIONS FOR ELDERLY OR DISABLED PERSONS
ALLOCATION OF APPRAISED VALUE OF DIVIDED UNIT
COST
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable value, lowering school district M&O tax revenue and final tax collections that §49.153 usesto compute the cost of attendance credit. No direct textual conflict, but fiscal calculations under this section are affected, requiring rulemaking updates.
nodes.quote: The cost of attendance credit for a school district is computed using the final tax collections of the district.

nodes.heading: PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DETACHMENT AND ANNEXATION
DETACHMENT OF PROPERTY
nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable values statewide, altering parcel taxable values used in detachment calculations and potentially shifting which districts exceed the §48.257 local revenue level, requiring consequential adjustments to wealth-equalization mechanics.
nodes.quote: The commissioner shall detach from each school district covered by Subsection (a) one or more whole parcels or items of property in descending order of the taxable value of each parcel or item

nodes.conflict_rationale: State health contribution is distributed through school finance formulas (Ch. 48 & 49, Educ. Code). Raising the homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue feeding those formulas, potentially requiring rulemaking to adjust distribution mechanics, though the $900 per-employee amount is unaffected.
nodes.quote: The state contribution shall be distributed through the school finance formulas under Chapters 48 and 49, Education Code, and used by school districts and charter schools as provided by Section 48.275, Education Code.

nodes.conflict_rationale: Section 11.261 freezes taxes based on first-year tax amount when individual qualified under Section 11.13(c). Increasing the Section 11.13(b) exemption alters the base tax calculation for new qualifiers also receiving the elderly/disabled exemption, requiring rulemaking guidance but no statutory amendment to Section 11.261.
nodes.quote: the county, municipality, or junior college district may not increase the total annual amount of ad valorem taxes the county, municipality, or junior college district imposes on the residence homestead of a disabled individual or an individual 65 years of age or older above the amount of the taxes the county, municipality, or junior college district imposed on the residence homestead in the first tax year

nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the exemption amount requires the comptroller to update the prescribed explanation content and application forms to reflect the new $200,000 threshold. This is a routine rulemaking update, not a structural conflict.
nodes.quote: The comptroller shall prescribe by rule the content of the explanation required by Subsection (a) of this section, and shall require that each exemption application form be printed and prepared:

nodes.conflict_rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable property values, which affects the denominator (Current Total Value - New Property Value) in the §26.0443 rate-adjustment formulas, indirectly altering computed rate increases for eligible county hospital expenditures.
nodes.quote: (Current Tax Year's Eligible County Hospital Expenditures - Preceding Tax Year's Eligible County Hospital Expenditures) / (Current Total Value - New Property Value)

nodes.conflict_rationale: §26.08(n)'s voter-approval tax rate calculation uses taxable value and references Ed. Code §48.2551 (compressed rate) and §45.0032 (enrichment rate). Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable values, creating a transition-year mismatch between preceding-year and current-year calculations requiring conforming amendments.
nodes.quote:
(n) For purposes of this section, the voter-approval tax rate of a school district is the sum of the following:
(1) the rate per $100 of taxable value that is equal to the district's maximum compressed tax rate, as determined under Section 48.2551, Education Code, for the current year;
(2) the greater of:
(A) the district's enrichment tax rate for the preceding tax year; or
(B) the rate of $0.05 per $100 of taxable value; and
(3) the district's current debt rate.
